verification_steps: Open CSV exports in grading environment and confirm derived columns match runbook values.
Check the Sensitivity sheet records for at least the baseline and +/-5% driver perturbation cases.
Confirm Remediation Recommendations table lists only budget-feasible combos and that totals match cost inputs.
Confirm grader/verifier run yields no internal-consistency issues (I1 resolved).

priority_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'issue': 'Sensitivity testing implemented incorrectly (applied to observed_risk instead of input drivers) and remediation recommendation visibility insufficient for external verification.'}

planned_changes.description: Replace current approach (multiplying observed_risk by 0.95/1.05) with driver perturbation rows that apply +/-5% to scope_weight, last_used_hours, and rotation_score individually and in pairwise/combined scenarios. Recompute scope_norm, last_used_norm, rotation_risk, observed_risk, margin, and class_code for each perturbation case.

Add columns that compare baseline class_code to each perturbed-case class_code and set an explicit 'needs_expert_review' boolean if any perturbation changes the classification. Document the review trigger rule in the runbook.

Create a Remediation Recommendations table that enumerates budget-feasible option combinations, per-record post-remediation classes, total_cost_usd, and a 'recommended' boolean. Ensure combinations exceeding remediation_budget_usd are excluded or clearly flagged. Mirror this table in the runbook (full, non-truncated) and reference exact cell ranges.
planned_changes.step: Enhance Remediation sheet and runbook outputs

planned_changes.description: Export Model, Sensitivity, and Remediation sheets as CSV files and include a 'formula_audit.txt' listing exact formulas used for each derived column (cell-level formulas or pseudocode). Add these files to artifacts and reference them in actual_outputs.json and runbook.

Untruncate containment_runbook.md: include full sensitivity tables, sample calculations for one record and one perturbation, remediation recommendation table, and explicit mapping from workbook outputs to runbook statements.
Record the CSV export and formula audit steps in agent_trace.json and include full stdout/stderr. Ensure actual_outputs.json lists the additional files so graders can preview them.

Submit revised artifacts to the grading pipeline and verify grader JSON is internally consistent: rubric item scores should map deterministically to the final_score and 'passed'/'failed_criteria' lists must not contradict each other. If the grader uses the workbook to grade, attach the CSV/formula_audit to avoid parser issues.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Address incorrect sensitivity testing (must perturb input drivers), improve artifact verifiability for graders lacking openpyxl, make remediation recommendations explicit and budget-filtered, and fix grader metadata inconsistencies so final score is reliable.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Work artifacts meet the artifact-contract (files present and documented) but contain two classes of problems: (1) a substantive correctness gap in sensitivity testing (sensitivity was applied to observed_risk rather than to the input drivers: scope_weight, last_used_hours, rotation_score) which violates the rubric's sensitivity requirement; and (2) verifier-detected metadata/internal-consistency problems in the grader outputs (contradictory pass/fail flags and mismatch between rubric item scores and final_score) that make the reported final score and pass status unreliable. Additionally, graders could not programmatically open the .xlsx in the environment; supply a verifier-friendly alternative (CSV or plaintext formula audit) to enable robust external verification.
